and certainly I would support any inclusion of those
who served in New Zealand, um,
during the New Zealand Awards from both sides.
All right. Thank you very much for your
submission this afternoon.
Do we have questions? Yeah, Tim Ley.
Um, thank you for your submission
and the detail, which you went through the recent one.
I found it really helpful, especially your
reflections on the speeches.
It's, uh, much appreciated and I,
and I take the points you make in there.
I guess my question as you touched on it just at the end,
that we, we've heard from some of the view that this,
the way the legislation is written, it says, in,
in commemoration of all those
who have served New Zealand in time of war,
Anzac Day will be a commemoration, uh,
a day of commemoration.
And it's not specifically about recognizing some would
argue any individual.
It's about commemorating the service and conflict.
And we've heard from others it should be anyone
that died in uniform, whether
that was in a conflict zone or not.
And then the separate issue of
how do you recognize splitting out the recognition piece
into a separate rec um, veteran's Day.
Now, I, I heard you, you touched on that at the end, said,
well, it's just another day.
But do you think a, an all inclusive day
that recognizes every veteran are those like yourself who,
who as you say, and, and I thank you for your service.
'cause you put on the uniform, you turned up,
you did your training, and it was just at the whim
of the government, who, who was sent and who wasn't,
and when, when, you know, uh, a bit of a time and place.
So, um, does, does that help to recognize
all those who have served New Zealand having a separate
Veteran's Day, whether or not maybe they were in the 1980s
and New Zealand wasn't going anywhere.
Does that help?
I think the, the problem you've got there is that already
going around the traps listening to people, we've got,
oh yes, there, there might be a, a Veteran's Day,
but I'm a war veteran and you are not.
Mm. So there's differentiation already taking place in
people's minds, and we've got this day that's carry
so much impact in Anzac Day.
It's an appropriate time, in my view, in the view of our,
our committee that we recognize the, um, the service
of all service people.
All right. Well thank you very
much for your time this afternoon.
We really appreciate you making the effort
to submit and to speak to it.
Thank you. Thank you. Thank you.
Alright, that brings an end
to the public hearings on the Anzac a Amendment bill.
